items.title: Defence weighs AGC appeal for driver in fatal Klang crash
items.summary: KLANG, July 22 — The defence is exploring a formal representation to the Attorney-General’s Chambers (AGC) for a technician accused of driving under the influence and causing a fatal crash that killed a delivery rider. The move comes as the 28-year-old accused, R. Saktygaanapathy, pleaded not guilty to a charge of self-administering tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) at the Klang Selatan district police headquarters on March 29. While the drug charge is being contested, Saktygaanapathy faces a far more severe legal battle.

items.title: Drug syndicates using last-minute airport check-ins to evade detection, say police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: International drug syndicates are exploiting last-minute and peak-hour airport check-ins to slip drug mules past authorities. Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said syndicates deliberately exploit busy periods in the hope of evading airport security and screening systems. Hussein said monitoring shows airports remain the primary route for drug mules, particularly those involved with international syndicates.

items.title: Kuala Pilah traders seek jobs, growth over election promises
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: As political parties criss-cross Negri Sembilan in search of votes ahead of the state election, traders in Kuala Pilah are hoping less for political rhetoric and more for meaningful change that will revive the district's economy and ease the rising cost of living. She has been operating her business for 16 years, including the past 12 years at her current premises near the Kuala Pilah bus terminal. Mira said Kuala Pilah's commercial centre no longer enjoyed the lively atmosphere it once did, with many long-established shops remaining shuttered as traders struggled with rising operating costs and competition from online sellers.

items.title: Berjasa's Zamani vows to improve housing, education, and healthcare in Nilai
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Berjasa candidate for the Nilai seat Zamani Ibrahim has pledged to champion affordable housing, local jobs, and better education and healthcare if elected. The party's sole candidate in the Negri Sembilan election, the 62-year-old said development in the fast-growing constituency must bring greater benefits to its residents. He said one of his priorities was ensuring development in Nilai directly benefited residents, particularly through affordable housing.

items.title: Rising costs, medical brain drain top agenda at APHM conference
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Association of Private Hospitals of Malaysia (APHM) will host its 32nd International Healthcare Conference and Exhibition from July 28 to 30, 2026, at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre (KLCC). In a statement, APHM said the conference comes at a critical juncture as global healthcare systems, including Malaysia's, grapple with escalating costs, a "brain drain" of medical professionals and mounting pressure to maintain affordability. This focus aligns with APHM's ongoing priority to address rising medical costs.

items.title: Pemadam: Rehab, not arrest numbers, the true measure in war on drugs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Requiring every police station chief to arrest at least one drug addict each day is a proactive move that deserves praise, but it should not be the sole benchmark in the fight against drugs. Malaysian Association for the Prevention of Drugs (Pemadam) secretary-general Hasnul Fairuz Hushin Amri said the true measure of success was not the number of arrests made, but whether drug abuse was declining and syndicates were being dismantled. "Combating drug abuse requires the development of a strong and sustainable ecosystem.

items.title: Amirudin: Pakatan banks on micro-campaign strategy to win over Negeri Sembilan voters
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is focusing on a locality-based micro-campaign strategy to deliver the coalition’s message and election pledges directly to grassroots voters in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. “In Negeri Sembilan, PH is leading the state government... the stability demonstrated during its first term will be a key selling point. In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election, PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14 and PN five.

items.title: Network School shifts to Kazakhstan after Johor authorities halt operations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Network School, which previously operated in Forest City, Johor, is set to establish a new campus in Kazakhstan. In a post on X, founder Balaji Srinivasan announced that a memorandum of understanding (MoU) had been signed between Network School and the Republic of Kazakhstan to develop the campus. In an Instagram post, Kazakhstan's Ministry of Digital Development, Innovation and Aerospace Industry said the MoU was signed by Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Digital Development, Innovation and Aerospace Industry Zhaslan Madiyev and Balaji.

items.title: Madani economy on right track as Malaysia moves up competitiveness rankings - Samenta
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's climb of eight places to 15th in the International Institute for Management Development (IMD) World Competitiveness Ranking demonstrates that the MADANI Economy framework is on the right track, particularly through the government's commitment to reducing bureaucratic red tape. Small and Medium Enterprises Association of Malaysia (SAMENTA) president Datuk William Ng said the country's best performance in more than a decade highlights the economy's true potential when the public and private sectors work closely together. "This is reflected in government efficiency rising 11 places to 14th globally, while business efficiency climbed 16 places to 16th in the world," he said in a statement here today.

items.title: Malaysia's AI ambitions require skilled ict workforce - Khairul Firdaus
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Malaysia's growing investments in artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing and hyperscale data centres are driving demand for a highly skilled information and communications technology (ICT) workforce, says Deputy Minister of Human Resources Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan. In this regard Khairul Firdaus said the Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma) continued to strengthen Malaysia's workforce by equipping Malaysians with the skills needed to thrive in an increasingly digital and AI-driven economy. The conference brings together ICT infrastructure professionals, engineers, consultants, technology providers, policymakers and business leaders from across Southeast Asia to discuss AI-ready infrastructure, advanced fibre connectivity, structured cabling, edge computing, cybersecurity, smart buildings and sustainable digital infrastructure.

items.title: Malaysia set to lead global ESG sukuk issuance in 2H26: Fitch
items.summary: Fitch Ratings said Malaysia-based issuers accounted for 67 per cent of global ESG sukuk issuance in the first half of 2026. Its share of dollar-denominated emerging market ESG debt, excluding China, also fell to 14 per cent from 41 per cent a year earlier, as dollar ESG sukuk issuance plunged 74 per cent while dollar ESG bond issuance rose 11.5 per cent. Despite weaker issuance, outstanding ESG sukuk rose about 24 per cent to more than US$61 billion at end-June 2026.

items.title: 44,018 complaints against police recorded from 2020 to 2025
items.summary: He said the largest number of complaints and information was received by Bukit Aman, with 27,566 cases, followed by the Kuala Lumpur police contingent with 2,111 and Selangor with 2,082. "Integrity-related complaints were the highest recorded during the period, with 23,186 complaints, followed by governance-related complaints (5,229), corruption-related complaints (894) and general complaints (14,709)," he said in a written parliamentary reply. "The remaining 35,974 complaints were brought for further investigation by the police," he said.

items.title: Sabah insists border control law must not erode state immigration powers
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The implementation of the Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency Act 2024 (Act 860) in Sabah must not erode the state's autonomous immigration powers. Assistant Minister to the Chief Minister Datuk Joniston Bangkuai said the Act falls under the federal government's jurisdiction. However, he said the state government maintains that Sabah's immigration powers, as enshrined under Article V of the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), are non-negotiable, as the formation of Malaysia was subject to the preservation of Sabah's and Sarawak's immigration authority.

items.title: Court of Appeal rules children born out of wedlock cannot inherit Malaysian father’s citizenship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Court of Appeal today unanimously decided that two persons who were born in Malaysia are not entitled to be Malaysian citizens under the Federal Constitution, as their biological Malaysian fathers were not married to their non-Malaysian mothers when they were born. Here’s a quick recap of the facts in their cases based on court documents, and what the Court of Appeal decided today: The Court of Appeal said Malaysia-born children have to follow their mother's citizenship status, if the child is illegitimate or born when the parents were not married. — File picture by Devan Manuel The case of J: Birth certificate once recorded her as Malaysian, had a Malaysian passport J, who will be turning 30 this year, has a DNA test to show her biological ties to her Malaysian father.

items.title: Psychological first aid vital to calm those going through crisis
items.summary: During an emergency, some people cry uncontrollably and others become silent. Yet, Yet very few people are taught what happens psychologically during a crisis. Communities look to leaders.

items.title: Govt aligns investment direction of GLICs, public funds through GEAR-uP, MoF Inc
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is aligning the investment direction of government-linked investment companies (GLICs) and public funds through a clear national development policy, the Government-linked Enterprises Activation and Reform Programme (GEAR-uP) mechanism, and the strategic role of the Minister of Finance Incorporated. Finance deputy minister Liew Chin Tong said this alignment is carried out without undermining each institution's investment mandate, governance, and fiduciary responsibilities. Liew was responding to Senator Datuk Rosni Sohar's question regarding the government's policy and mechanisms for aligning the investment direction of GLICs and public funds with national development priorities.

items.title: Govt aligns investment direction of GLICs, public funds through GEAR uP, MOF Inc
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is aligning the investment direction of government-linked investment companies (GLICs) and public funds through a clear national development policy, the Government-linked Enterprises Activation and Reform Programme (GEAR-uP) mechanism, and the strategic role of the Minister of Finance Incorporated (MOF Inc). Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said this alignment is carried out without undermining each institution's investment mandate, governance, and fiduciary responsibilities. Liew was responding to Senator Datuk Rosni Sohar's question regarding the government's policy and mechanisms for aligning the investment direction of GLICs and public funds with national development priorities.

items.title: Fancy winning RM3,500 a month for life?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd has brought back its "Gaji Seumur Hidup" campaign for a third edition, strengthening consumer engagement while continuing its support for charitable causes. The campaign, which runs from Aug 1 to Sept 30, offers prizes worth more than RM4 million. Nestlé Malaysia will also contribute RM900,000 to 21 charitable organisations nationwide under this year's campaign.

items.title: UK's Aberdeen Group emerges as substantial shareholder in MTT Shipping
items.summary: Bursa Malaysia filings showed that abrdn Malaysia Sdn Bhd purchased 126.86 million shares in MTT Shipping on July 20, representing a 5.075 per cent direct interest. According to Bursa Malaysia shareholding data, individual investors collectively own 53.10 per cent of MTT Shipping, while corporate shareholders account for 21.44 per cent. He is followed by Malaysia Trade and Transport Company Sdn Bhd, which owns 14.66 per cent, or 366.40 million shares.

items.title: Bangkok pub fire death toll rises to 35, medical centre says
items.summary: BANGKOK: The death toll from a July 13 fire at a pub in the Thai capital Bangkok rose to 35 on Wednesday, the city's emergency services said. Twenty-one people were still in hospital, with 12 still in critical condition, the Erawan Emergency Medical Centre said. On Monday, Thai police said no charges had been filed because the venue's owner was still in hospital.

items.title: MediAsas to complement, not replace, public healthcare system - Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming MediAsas medical and health insurance/takaful plan will complement, rather than replace, the public healthcare system, which will continue to provide Universal Health Coverage (UHC) for Malaysians. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the plan was being developed as part of the Government's RESET Strategy to address medical claims inflation and improve the sustainability of healthcare financing. "Importantly, the MediAsas plan currently being developed does not replace but complements the public healthcare system, which will continue to provide Universal Health Coverage (UHC) for all Malaysians," he said in a written parliamentary reply.

items.title: Fatal Klang crash: Defence lawyer mulls representation to A-GC
items.summary: Defence lawyer Aravind Raj said he would first review all prosecution documents relating to his client R. Saktygaanapathy's charge of self-administering drugs before deciding on the proposed representation. A representation is typically submitted to seek a withdrawal or reduction of the charge against an accused. Saktygaanapathy is accused of causing Amirul's death along Jalan Raya Barat here at 11.47am on March 29.

items.title: Anwar hails federal-state cooperation in driving Sarawak’s growth and development
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the close cooperation and shared commitment between the Federal Government and the Sarawak Government continue to strengthen the state’s position as a leader in growth, innovation and inclusive development. In a Facebook post marking Sarawak Day today, Anwar said every initiative undertaken reflects a shared determination to uplift the people, strengthen the economy and ensure lasting prosperity. “Happy Sarawak Day to all Sarawakians,” he said.

items.title: Sarawak Day: Anwar lauds progress, reaffirms federal ties
items.summary: In his Sarawak Day message today, Anwar said every effort was being driven towards elevating the people's livelihoods, strengthening the economy and ensuring sustainable prosperity. He said the transformation and progress taking place in Sarawak were the result of collective efforts, with the spirit of Sarawak Maju Segulai Sejalai continuing to drive unity, development and a more prosperous future for the state and Malaysia. "Through the spirit of Sarawak Maju Segulai Sejalai, we will continue to move forward together in strengthening unity, accelerating development and building a more prosperous future for Sarawak and Malaysia," he said.

items.title: #NSTViral: Kuantan beach turns magical as rare bioluminescent waves draw crowds [WATCH]
items.summary: KUANTAN: A stunning bioluminescent phenomenon has drawn large crowds to Pantai Tembeling here as visitors flock to catch a glimpse of its unique glowing waves. Social media has been abuzz with images of the natural spectacle, which causes stretches of the shoreline to appear luminous due to bioluminescent plankton in the water. The mesmerising phenomenon of bioluminescence has now been reported near Tembeling Beach, Kuantan.

items.title: Tremors felt along west coast after Sumatra quake [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tremors were felt along the west coast of Peninsular Malaysia following a moderate earthquake in northern Sumatra today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the 5.7-magnitude earthquake occurred at 12.18pm, with the epicentre located at coordinates 4.2° North and 97.6° East. "The earthquake was recorded 55km southwest of Langsa, Indonesia, at a depth of 10km," it said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Noor'azah determined to champion women's voices in Ampangan
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Bersatu's Ampangan seat candidate Noor'azah Harun is determined to champion and amplify the voices of women in the constituency if elected. "I know many women, particularly single mothers, are reluctant to come forward. She said several issues had been raised by residents, including complaints from traders at Benteng Ampangan.

items.title: Cattle herder remanded for four days over alleged police assault
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: A 43-year-old cattle herder has been remanded for four days to assist investigations into allegedly obstructing and assaulting a policeman. Magistrate Chia Huey Ting issued the remand order until July 25 to facilitate investigations under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant in the discharge of their public functions. The incident allegedly occurred yesterday at the Balik Pulau public market when a police Mobile Patrol Vehicle team spotted the suspect's motorcycle parked beside the food court while he was behaving suspiciously.

items.title: WD Malaysia bets on sustainable storage as AI data demand surges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nasdaq-listed Western Digital Corp (WDC) is stepping up efforts to make artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure more sustainable as global demand for data storage accelerates, focusing on higher storage efficiency, lower energy consumption and reduced physical infrastructure requirements. Dr Chandramogan Anamirtham, senior vice-president of global operations at WDC, said responsible manufacturing has become increasingly important as demand for AI-driven data storage grows. "As global demand for AI-driven data storage accelerates, our commitment to responsible manufacturing has never been more critical," he said.

items.title: Did you feel it? MetMalaysia says 5.7 magnitude Sumatra quake rattles west coast of peninsula
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — A moderate earthquake in North Sumatra sent tremors rippling across the west coast of Peninsular Malaysia on Tuesday. The Malaysian Meteorological Department reported that the quake struck at 12:18 pm today, registering a magnitude of 5.7. The Meteorological Department is now urging anyone who experienced tremors to provide their feedback via an official survey form .

items.title: Higher oil prices may push petrol, diesel subsidies to RM3.5b monthly, says Liew Chin Tong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The government estimates that the RON95 petrol subsidy will reach approximately RM2 billion per month, while the diesel subsidy will amount to about RM1.5 billion per month, if crude oil prices remain at around US$90 per barrel based on current projections. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said that, so far, the country’s fiscal position remains strong and fiscal projections are unchanged despite the increase in crude oil prices. “The government also earns revenue from petroleum.

items.title: Illegal houses on state land rented to Rohingya for higher rents, says Kelantan exco
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Kelantan government believes many villagers who have illegally built houses on state land are renting them out to foreigners, including Rohingya, attracted by higher rental returns. Hilmi said enforcement action was only taken against illegal structures erected on government land. "Our action is limited to illegal structures built on government land.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Sri Tanjung independent candidate to appear in court over social media post
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Independent candidate contesting the Sri Tanjung seat in the Negeri Sembilan state election, Islah Wahyudi Zainudin, will have to appear before the Sepang Sessions Court on Aug 11 over a charge linked to a social media post. The 46-year-old candidate said he received a notice of the charge from an officer of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) yesterday. “Yes, an MCMC officer came to my house around midday yesterday.

items.title: Don't exploit divisive issues, Aminuddin warns political parties in Negri polls
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Political parties contesting the Negri Sembilan state election have been urged not to exploit issues that could undermine racial unity in the state. Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the harmony enjoyed by the multi-ethnic communities in the state must be preserved and not exploited for political gain. "Negri Sembilan has long enjoyed good understanding among its multi-racial communities.

items.title: Keropok seller charged with using drone to smuggle tobacco into prison
items.summary: MELAKA: A keropok seller was charged in the magistrate's court here with criminal trespass and smuggling tobacco into Sungai Udang Prison using an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), or drone, last week. On the first charge, Nizam is accused of committing criminal trespass with the intention to smuggle contraband tobacco into the Sungai Udang Prison compound using a drone under his control. On the second charge, Nizam is accused of smuggling prohibited tobacco into Sungai Udang Prison using a drone under his control at the same time, date, and location.

items.title: Police keep election campaign activities in check as Negeri Sembilan polls heat up
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — The authorities approved 173 out of 178 applications to hold talks and political campaign activities for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election between July 18 and yesterday. Police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said all approved programmes and political activities are being closely monitored by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) to ensure they stay within legal bounds and do not jeopardise public safety or order. Over the same period, he said, nine police reports were received, and five investigation papers opened, one of which, opened yesterday, involves an election offence.

items.title: NY mayor says city doesn't have authority to arrest Netanyahu on ICC warrant
items.summary: NEW YORK: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said Tuesday his office does not have the authority to carry out the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and called on US federal officials to step in. "My administration has reviewed every avenue available under applicable law to determine whether New York City could execute the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant if Benjamin Netanyahu came here," Mamdani said in a brief video message posted to social media. Netanyahu's office has dismissed Mamdani's stance and said the ICC is a kangaroo court.

items.title: #NSTViral: Rider slammed for performing wheelie with woman, child on motorcycle
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A motorcyclist has drawn widespread criticism after he was filmed performing a wheelie while carrying a woman and a child on his motorcycle at Alor Star City Square. The stunt angered social media users, particularly as the child appeared not to be wearing a helmet. Threads user @cerita.harini__, who uploaded the clip, sarcastically remarked that the rider had remembered to conceal the motorcycle's registration plate but appeared to have given little thought to the child's safety.

items.title: Keropok seller charged over drone tobacco smuggling attempt at Sungai Udang Prison
items.summary: The accused, Mohd Nizam Ab Manaf, 46, made the plea after the charges were read out before Magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman. For the first charge, Mohd Nizam is accused of committing criminal trespass with intent to smuggle prohibited items, namely tobacco, into Sungai Udang Prison using a drone. For the second charge, Mohd Nizam is accused of smuggling tobacco into Sungai Udang Prison using a drone at the same place, time and date.

items.title: Anwar’s Negeri Sembilan campaign debut aims to boost Pakatan election momentum
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will hit the campaign trail in Negeri Sembilan today in an effort to further strengthen Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) momentum in the ongoing state election. In the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, PH is contesting all 36 state seats, with 16 candidates from PKR, 11 from DAP and nine from Amanah. The Election Commission (EC) has set early voting for the Negeri Sembilan state election on July 28, while polling day is on Aug 1.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul celebrates daughter’s graduation in UK, hopes for her homecoming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — Former minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Aziz has couched his personal hopes in a celebration of his daughter’s academic achievement, urging her to return to Malaysia once she completes her studies. He made the reminder as he celebrated his daughter Zahra’s graduation from the University of Warwick. Zahra has distinguished herself academically, earning First Class Honours in English Literature.

items.title: Last mile of reform: Samenta calls for local govt overhaul to sustain Malaysia’s competitiveness gain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — While Malaysia has surged to a historic 15th in the IMD World Competitiveness Ranking, the Small and Medium Enterprises Association of Malaysia (Samenta) warns that these macro-level gains must now trickle down to the SME sector. To achieve this, the association urged the government to target the next critical layer of bureaucracy: local councils (PBTs) and statutory bodies. “SMEs consistently report that unpredictable processing timelines for licenses, permits and renewals as well as erratic enforcement remain a primary driver of rising operational costs and business uncertainties,” Samenta national president Datuk William Ng said in a statement.

items.title: MIC to hold nationwide Fun Run across 137 divisions for 80th anniversary celebrations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — MIC will hold a nationwide Fun Run this Sunday (July 26), with events rolling out simultaneously across all 137 of its divisions as part of the party’s 80th anniversary celebrations, said vice-president Datuk T. Murugiah. The main event, a 5km run, will be held at Taman Tasik Titiwangsa here, with MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran flagging off participants at 7 am. “We are organising the Fun Run not only to celebrate MIC’s 80th anniversary but also to foster unity among Malaysians of all races.

items.title: Tawau man who forced daughter to book rooms where he raped her gets 105 years’ prison
items.summary: TAWAU, July 21 — A 53-year-old man was sentenced to 105 years in prison and 70 strokes of the cane by the Tawau Sessions Court after pleading guilty to seven counts of raping his eldest daughter, in a case prosecutors described as premeditated and aggravated by emotional manipulation. Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the sentence immediately after the man pleaded guilty to all charges read against him. According to the charges, the man raped his biological daughter, now 25, at several hotels in Tawau between December 2025 and March 2026.

items.title: Sabah Southern Madani Link to strengthen state’s electricity supply security, says Petra
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Sabah Southern Madani Link (SSML) project by the Sabah state government is a strategic investment that strengthens the stability and security of the state’s electricity supply, according to the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (Petra). The ministry said the project also supports the integration of new electricity supply generation, improves the cost efficiency of the electrical system, and strengthens Sabah’s position in the regional energy integration agenda. The ministry said the strategic SSML project was implemented to reinforce Sabah’s 275-kilovolt electricity transmission system and plays an important role in ensuring power from existing and new generation sources can be delivered, particularly to the state’s east coast.

items.title: Hiker dies at Bukit Maras peak, body carried down by rescuers
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, July 22 — A man was reported to have died at the summit of Bukit Maras here yesterday evening. Kuala Nerus Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations commander Muhammad Al Fadil Mat Amin said in the 6.48 pm incident, the victim, Nazli A. Hamid, 55, was found unconscious at the hilltop and was pronounced dead by a medical team that arrived at the scene. “Upon receiving the emergency call, six personnel from the Kuala Nerus Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) were deployed to the location.

items.title: MetMalaysia sounds thunderstorm warning for Kedah, Penang, Perak, Johor, Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, forecasting heavy rain and strong winds across multiple regions this morning. The warning remains in effect until 11:00am today. The warning extends to Johor, specifically affecting Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Pontian, and Johor Bahru.

items.title: Sibling rivalry turns fatal after 29-year-old lorry driver killed in Kuala Kubu Bharu fight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — A dispute between two siblings in Kuala Kubu Bharu turned fatal yesterday, leaving a 29-year-old man dead. Hulu Selangor OCPD Supt Manimaran Muniandi said police were alerted to the incident at approximately 5:30 pm yesterday. The suspect, a 26-year-old sibling of the deceased, was apprehended by Hulu Selangor police shortly after the incident.

items.title: Four illegal houses demolished in Tanah Merah as Kelantan cracks down on land encroachment
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 22 — The Kelantan government has reiterated its zero-tolerance stance on state land encroachment following the demolition of four illegal houses in Pechong, Mukim Bandar Tanah Merah yesterday. The structures were illegally erected on property belonging to the Kelantan Land Commissioner (PTK) said state Local Government, Housing, Health, and Environment Committee chairman Hilmi Abdullah. “Four houses built without approval were demolished under the National Land Code 1965 to protect state land from any encroachment, usage, or development without approval,” he said when contacted today.

items.title: KL police move monthly assembly to night as crime, operations peak after dark
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — Kuala Lumpur police have introduced a new approach by holding their monthly assembly at night to reflect the reality of the force’s round-the-clock operations. The assembly, held at the Kuala Lumpur Police Contingent Headquarters, was attended by about 1,100 senior and junior police officers, as well as civilian staff. “By holding an assembly like this, it will make the culture of being active at night more aggressive, in line with ‘Kuala Lumpur that never sleeps’,” he told reporters after the Kuala Lumpur Police Chief’s Monthly Assembly here last night.

items.title: Nearly 100,000 jobs available through MYFutureJobs platform, says Ramanan
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 22 — A total of 96,700 job opportunities are still actively offered through the MYFutureJobs platform managed by the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) as of June 30. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said that a total of 668,321 job vacancies have been advertised via the platform. Ramanan said the statistics prove that job opportunities continue to be available across various sectors and skill levels.

items.title: After Johor, PAS issues mandate to back BN candidates in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: TAMPIN — PAS has issued a directive for its members to support Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in the Negeri Sembilan state election, signalling a deepening strategic cooperation between the two coalitions. PAS secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan urged the grassroots in the Tampin parliamentary constituency to turn out in force on August 1. He specifically called for support for both BN and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidates across three key state seats to ensure a decisive victory over Pakatan Harapan (PH), NST reported.

items.title: Anwar to kick off first Negeri Sembilan campaign tour with four events today
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 22 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to attend a series of campaign events at four locations in Seremban and Paroi today as part of the campaign for the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election. This will be Anwar’s first visit to Negeri Sembilan since the start of the state election campaign. For the latest news on the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9.

items.title: Hamzah: Perikatan to use ‘PBN’ label to convey BN partnership in Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: TAMPIN, July 222 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) has introduced the label “PBN” to describe its electoral pact with Barisan Nasional (BN) for the August 1 Negeri Sembilan state election. “What matters is our shared spirit and commitment to determine Negeri Sembilan’s future.” The event was attended by a high-profile lineup, including PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee, Tampin Umno division chief Datuk Mohd Isam Mohd Isa, Gemencheh BN candidate Suhaimizan Bizar, and Gemas PN candidate Ridzuan Ahmad. Hamzah confirmed that PN agreed from the outset for BN to lead the electoral pact in Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: MDEC to revoke digital status of Forest City school operator NSO Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 22 — The Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) is taking immediate steps to revoke the Malaysia Digital status of NSO Malaysia Sdn Bhd. NSO operates Network School in Forest City, Iskandar Puteri, Johor. “Accordingly, MDEC is taking immediate action to revoke NSO’s Malaysia Digital status,” it said.

items.title: Petaling Jaya’s leasehold dilemma: High premiums, shrinking buyers and ageing leases
items.summary: Currently, leasehold property owners in Petaling Jaya have two options: Option 1: A property owner can pay a nominal fee of RM5,000 and apply to extend the lease up to 99 years. Option 2: Should the property owner intend to sell the property, he or she has to renew the lease for 99 years and pay the full premium based on this formula: [¼ x 1/100 x Value of land x 99 years x Area of Land] The property owner is entitled to a 30 per cent discount, but the premium must be settled within six months after Form 5A for lease renewal is issued. Rajesh said many property leases in Sections 1 to 18 would expire in about 42 years, with some properties having only 20 or 25 years remaining.

items.title: PM Anwar calls on immigration personnel to uphold integrity, reject corruption
items.summary: In a Facebook post in conjunction with Immigration Day today, Anwar said that, in addition to upholding integrity, JIM personnel must always embrace the value of compassion in delivering efficient, courteous and professional services. “Happy Immigration Day to all personnel of the Immigration Department. The 2026 Immigration Day celebration is themed “Charting a New Face for Immigration”.

items.title: Sabah government says in midst of proposing upgrade of BUDI diesel subsidy scheme
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 21 — Citing the difficulties it has caused for people in the state, the state government intends to give its feedback and present new proposals to the federal government on the targeted diesel subsidy scheme that limits eligible recipients to a 200-litre monthly quota. State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said the many difficulties faced by Sabahans was the basis for establishing a special task force to review the scheme before formally submitting improvement proposals to the federal government. He said this in response to Likas assemblyman Tham Yun Fook who asked whether the Sabah government officially supported the current 200-litre diesel subsidy policy.

items.title: Sabah NGO president arrested by MACC over alleged RM2m fund misappropriation
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 21 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested the president of a Sabah-based non-governmental organisation (NGO) on suspicion of abusing his position by misappropriating about RM2 million allocated for the construction of a community hall. When contacted, Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah confirmed the arrest. Mohd Fuad added that the suspect is expected to be charged at the Kota Kinabalu Special Corruption Court on Thursday.

items.title: Sabah denies RM210m GLC allocation as bailout, instead strategic investments without new cashflow
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 21 — Sabah has defended its RM210 million allocation for state-linked companies, rejecting claims that the government was giving “bailouts” but instead said they were strategic investments that had undergone financial evaluation and risk assessment. State Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said the allocation under the Supplementary Supply Bill 2026 involved RM200 million for Sabah Development Bank Berhad (SDBank) and RM10 million for POIC Sabah Sdn Bhd. Masidi who is also deputy chief minister II said the RM200 million allocation for SDBank did not involve any new cash injection from the state government, but was instead a phased accounting adjustment to convert existing fixed deposits held by the government into redeemable preference shares (RPS).
